title: diasperus assamicus
side_effects: Diasperus assamicus can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased heart rate and blood pressure, which can lead to cardiovascular problems. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure may result in liver and kidney damage. Additionally, improper use of the plant has also been linked to allergic reactions, including skin rashes and respiratory issues. In severe cases, overdose can lead to seizures, coma, and even death.
